message:
fix(runtime): Use Ryu algorithm for %r format on small floats

Fix %r (repr) format for small floating-point numbers (< 1e-4).
The manual normalization loop (multiply by 10 until mantissa >= 1.0)
accumulated floating-point errors, causing numbers like
0.00009999999999999999 to be incorrectly formatted as 1e-04
instead of 9.999999999999999e-05.

Solution: Use Zig's {e} formatter (Ryu algorithm) to get the correct
mantissa representation, then reformat the exponent to Python's style
(always 2 digits with explicit sign, e.g., e-05 instead of e-5).
